name: AI Attendance – Low Performance Alert Handler

nodes.name: Receive Alert from Flask
nodes.notes: Flask posts here whenever a student falls below 40%. Set this URL as N8N_ALERT_WEBHOOK in your Flask environment.

nodes.name: Parse & Split Recipients
nodes.notes: Parses the Flask payload and splits into individual email items (one per recipient: student + parent)

nodes.name: Valid Email?
nodes.notes: Only proceed if the recipient has a valid email address

parameters.subject: =⚠️ Low Performance Alert – {{ $json.student }}

nodes.name: Send Alert Email
nodes.notes: Configure your SMTP credentials in n8n (Gmail/Outlook/etc.). The email is sent separately to student and parent.

nodes.name: Log Alert to Google Sheet
nodes.notes: Logs every alert to an 'Alerts Log' sheet in Google Sheets for record-keeping. Create this sheet tab in your spreadsheet.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ received: true, student: $('Parse & Split Recipients').first().json.student, alerts_count: $('Parse & Split Recipients').first().json.alerts.length }) }}

nodes.name: Respond OK to Flask
nodes.notes: Sends a 200 OK back to Flask so it knows the alert was received

_comment: SETUP STEPS: 1) Import this JSON into n8n  2) Configure SMTP credentials (Gmail App Password recommended)  3) Set your Google Sheet ID and add an 'Alerts Log' sheet tab  4) Activate the workflow  5) Copy the Webhook URL and set it as N8N_ALERT_WEBHOOK environment variable in Flask
